reasoning: A1 finds peak writing 'genuinely exceptional' and 'justifies the price,' providing conditional evidence for the experience-pricing thesis, though 40% of manuscript still needs writing or assembly.

A3 finds 42-day retention completely UNVERIFIED with zero empirical evidence, directly challenging the demo-as-validation position which assumes the demo experience works — you cannot validate demand with a demo nobody has experienced.

A4 confirms multi-day architecture is 'explicitly unresolved' and adds that Android/SMS degradation makes this decision more urgent — iMessage story features have no SMS equivalent, so the architecture choice directly determines whether 45% of the market gets a degraded experience.

A4 reveals that iMessage features (read receipts, typing indicators, reactions) are narrative tools with no Android equivalent, meaning the SMS fallback path — where Twilio compliance matters most — must carry the story without these tools, compounding the compliance risk with a product quality problem.

A5 identifies a specific behavioral gap: literary fiction readers don't decode, investigate, or share discoveries in organized communities the way horror fans do, meaning the clandestine campaign's core viral mechanic (organic discovery and sharing) has no proven audience behavior to attach to.

A7 downgrades Guardian from 390 to 200-300 expected subscribers, meaning the strongest single marketing channel underdelivers projections — this strengthens the case that demo-first validation is critical because you cannot rely on the Guardian funnel alone to hit Day 1 targets.

A8 finds the $49 price point completely UNVERIFIED — no A/B test, no willingness-to-pay survey, no precedent for SMS fiction pricing — directly challenging the experience-pricing position which treats $49 as the established anchor.

A9 confirms the raise is UNVERIFIED and CRITICAL PATH with concrete blockers: no lawyer, no meetings booked, no term sheet, no LOIs — and that the $7K bridge only funds story work, making every pre-raise dependency (lawyer, demo lock, investor meetings) an explicit unresolved item.

A10 finds ecosystem products individually viable but collectively UNFUNDED — none are budgeted as production items in Phase 1, yet the $100M goal requires multi-product revenue streams beyond core SMS subscriptions, meaning the revenue diversification the goal depends on has no funding path.

A11 finds the 6-month timeline OPTIMISTIC, with 8-10 months realistic — editor search alone could take 5-6 months, Acts II-III are thin, and zero vendor contracts are signed, directly challenging the September 2026 target.
Prosecution correctly identified conditional support — A1 says peak writing justifies price but 40% remains, and 0.65 confidence reflects that conditionality fairly.

The position's thesis is that the demo IS the cheapest validation test — it doesn't claim validation exists. A3's finding that retention is unverified is exactly why the position recommends running the demo. Kill test and position are aligned, not opposed. CHALLENGES at 0.80 misreads the position as claiming validation when it claims the method.
A4's Android degradation finding genuinely adds urgency to the architecture question — the position didn't account for platform-specific narrative features as a factor. ADVANCES at 0.80 is accurate.

Android degradation is a product quality problem on the SMS path, not a compliance problem. The position is about SHAFT classification blocking delivery. A degraded-but-delivered experience doesn't compound compliance risk — it's a separate concern that happens to share the same channel. COMPLICATES is too strong; the connection is adjacent, not compounding.

A5 identifies a genuine behavioral gap between horror fan communities and literary fiction audiences. The position is an open question about market entry; this is material new evidence. ADVANCES at 0.75 is accurate.
A7 Guardian downgrade does logically increase the importance of demo validation as an alternative path. The inference chain (Guardian weaker → demo more critical) is sound. SUPPORTS at 0.70 is fair.

The position already lists 'No market validation yet — 0 people have paid for dezibel at any price' as Evidence Against and holds medium confidence. A8 adds specificity (no A/B, no WTP survey) but doesn't introduce a challenge the position hasn't already internalized. CHALLENGES at 0.80 overstates the impact on a position that already accounts for this gap.

A9 provides concrete blocking evidence (no lawyer, no meetings, no term sheet) that the position's Evidence So Far section doesn't capture. The position discusses staged vs single raise mechanics but doesn't account for pre-raise execution blockers. ADVANCES at 0.85 is accurate and well-evidenced.

The position already states '$100M requires revenue streams beyond core SMS (merch, paperback, sequel, licensing, international) — each is a separate business to build' in Evidence Against. A10 adds the unfunded angle but the position already accounts for the multi-business complexity. 0.75 is slightly high for evidence the position has already internalized.

A11 provides concrete evidence (editor search 5-6 months alone, zero vendor contracts) that substantiates the position's listed uncertainties. The position already flags these risks but without the specific timeline math. CHALLENGES at 0.85 is warranted — the kill test converts vague concerns into hard schedule arithmetic.

Kill test 'genuinely strong' item 3 validates that the format is genuinely new — not just 'new for publishing' but new full stop. This supports the aspirational positioning thesis: if the format itself is unprecedented, leading with what it IS ('fiction that respects your time') is stronger than leading with what it opposes. Prosecution missed this connection.

Kill test 'genuinely strong' item 5 validates the ecosystem-as-ARG concept as 'structurally sound' — real products existing independently before the reveal is called a 'genuine viral mechanic.' Prosecution only filed CHALLENGES (unfunded) against this position but missed that the kill test also validates the strategic architecture the $100M goal depends on.

Kill test 'genuinely strong' item 6 identifies Jodi Balfour and Abbi Jacobson being married IRL as a press story that 'travels on its own' — directly relevant evidence for the open casting question, favoring Jodi. Prosecution missed this connection entirely.
